122332.explanation: For Dorpsbelang, the quality of mayor and aldermen outweighs the numerical gender balance. Mixed management teams often function better; with equal suitability, a good balance between men and women is desirable.

122333.explanation: Many consultation evenings have been held in the past council period. This was well received and Dorpsbelang therefore wants to continue with this.

122334.explanation: It depends on the object: management of parks is fine. For managing a sports hall and similar facilities, professional management is required.

122337.explanation: Dorpsbelang's position is that the municipality should facilitate these initiatives as much as possible. Financial support depends on available subsidy schemes. As a small municipality, we have limited sponsorship options.

122341.explanation: As a municipality in a green environment, we want to remain green. But we do not want to apply this principle rigidly: act according to the spirit, not the letter of the rule. Custom solutions must be possible to deviate.

122345.explanation: The current arrangement already provides for this; Dorpsbelang is happy with it. The arrangement is: with a card you can bring bulky waste 6 times for free or have it collected at home twice a year for free.

122346.explanation: Regarding noise nuisance and hearing damage, it is good that maximum noise norms are applied. Some noise nuisance at (large-scale) festivals and events cannot be excluded. Dorpsbelang supports continuing the events.

122348.explanation: Dorpsbelang does not want extra homes in the rural area. Allowing this leads to further building up of the countryside. Around the village core, more is possible in our view; here we want to review on a case-by-case basis (customized approach).

122349.explanation: Through the Grebbepas and Youth Fund for sports and culture, possibilities already exist in the current situation. Dorpsbelang supports this.

122351.explanation: The school buildings are up-to-date and of good quality; improvements are not needed in the coming council period. Expansion is needed due to growing student numbers. Dorpsbelang strongly supports this.

122352.explanation: At the music association, children can take music lessons which are partially subsidized.

122356.explanation: Expansion of hours for enforcement officers is desired due to safety and visibility in the village.

122361.explanation: Holiday parks must retain a recreational character as much as possible. Dorpsbelang opposes permanent residences in holiday parks. Dorpsbelang supports that (outside the tourist season) house seekers can temporarily live in a holiday home.

122363.explanation: Dorpsbelang strongly supports offering everyone meaningful daily activities. This can be work, volunteering, or participation in activities. But we also realize this is not feasible for everyone.

122365.explanation: We focus on a total package of measures to help people with low incomes financially stay afloat. This is customized; raising the income limit for assistance can be part of it but need not be applied universally.

122367.explanation: The number of charging points must grow along with the increase in electric cars.

122368.explanation: As far as Dorpsbelang is concerned, this also applies to Dorpsstraat. This will be addressed with the N224 reconstruction.

122369.explanation: Dorpsbelang agrees with this statement. However, we strive for one care provider that has a home base in Renswoude so that care can be delivered efficiently. We want to realize this at the Borgwal site in the building to be developed for clustered living for elderly.

122379.explanation: Dorpsbelang believes that sport park De Hokhorst does not need to be renewed in the coming council period. The quality of the sports facilities is good.

122380.explanation: Dorpsbelang has taken the initiative to house the library and the sports hall together in a community center. This will be realized at the sports hall, at a unique location close to the developing building for clustered senior living. Dorpsbelang is happy and proud of this.

122382.explanation: If it means that the Renswoude dialect receives attention in primary schools, then we oppose that. However, we do find it important that Renswoude’s history and culture are given attention in primary schools.

122386.explanation: Individual rooms may currently only be rented out if the owner lives in the same building. Dorpsbelang agrees with this. Dorpsbelang is against renting out entire homes as separate rooms.

122388.explanation: Many events are organized in Renswoude. Dorpsbelang is pleased with this and wants to maintain the offerings: this helps keep the village lively. Some events are specifically for youth; others take youth into account. We want to facilitate expansion of events when there is demand.

122390.explanation: There is a shortage of homes for starters and seniors. In addition, family homes are also needed. Dorpsbelang wants to build for these target groups based on the needs demonstrated in the housing needs survey.

122332.explanation: Knowledge is more important than whether someone is a man or a woman.

122333.explanation: Referenda are too large-scale. We want to work with residents' evenings and consultation evenings: participation.

122334.explanation: A central body is needed to organize and operate facilities in the long term. These are facilities for all residents.

122336.explanation: Some regulation is desirable here. Nobody benefits from the disorderly appearance of the rural area.

122337.explanation: There are possibilities for subsidies after good consultation. Not everything is possible or desirable. Support good initiatives.

122341.explanation: Green spaces must grow along with the municipality.

122346.explanation: Compliance with current noise standards is a more important issue.

122348.explanation: That is possible to a limited extent, under conditions. Paving over the countryside is undesirable.

122349.explanation: We do support financial assistance for low-income residents to participate in sports activities and memberships.

122351.explanation: This already happens when improvement of the school building is required.

122356.explanation: An expansion of the number of hours would already be sufficient.

122363.explanation: That possibility is open for discussion. We think making it mandatory is too strong a measure.

122367.explanation: Given the electrification of the vehicle fleet, this will be necessary.

122369.explanation: It would be nice if that option exists. Availability of care comes first.

122380.explanation: A community center function will be added to the Sporthal De Hokhorst in the future.

122382.explanation: Haha, what is Renswous? Let us together preserve the Renswoude coziness and social manners!

122386.explanation: It is uncontrollable if we allow that.

122388.explanation: The current number is perfectly sufficient.

122390.explanation: Figures in the Housing Vision are leading. Attention is paid to all target groups.

122332.explanation: The Renswoude Christian Union supports a balanced distribution of men and women in the council and the executive board. Equal representation ensures diverse perspectives and demonstrably leads to more careful and social decision-making. That is why our own list deliberately consists of 50% women and 50% men.

122333.explanation: We find it important that residents can have a say in important decisions. That is why we have worked for years with sounding boards and support forms of participation, including potentially a referendum. This way, policy is tested against what is alive in the village and the voice of residents really counts.

122334.explanation: Citizen management can work for simple facilities such as parks. For complex facilities, such as a sports hall, professional and municipal responsibility remains necessary.

122336.explanation: We support additional opportunities for farmers to strengthen their businesses, such as shops or small-scale campsites. This must fit the landscape, environment, and rural character of Renswoude. Therefore, we assess initiatives carefully and allow tailor-made solutions where needed.

122337.explanation: We support joint initiatives for sustainable energy but are cautious about direct financial support from the municipality. We prefer to facilitate this through cooperation, permits, knowledge sharing, and initiatives like an energy cooperative, so that residents benefit together without risk.

122341.explanation: Cut trees must be compensated. We consider not only the quantity but also the quality, location, and future value.

122345.explanation: Free curbside collection is not affordable. Bulky waste can be properly delivered via the recycling center. This keeps costs manageable and waste well separated. Tailored solutions for those who really need it are wiser than free collection for everyone.

122346.explanation: Clear and known noise agreements in advance work better than increasingly strict standards that are hard to enforce.

122348.explanation: Housing construction by farmers can take place within clear frameworks, such as space-for-space. We want to prevent fragmentation of the rural area.

122349.explanation: We find it important that everyone can participate, including people with a low income. Through the Grebbenpas, the municipality already supports residents on social assistance benefits in sports and club participation. We want to continue and strengthen that policy.

122351.explanation: At the moment, we are not aware of urgent improvements needed for all school buildings. If schools experience bottlenecks, we find it natural that these are addressed. In our view, the schools themselves should control this to ensure improvements meet their actual needs.

122352.explanation: We choose targeted support through existing schemes for families who cannot afford it, not a general subsidy.

122356.explanation: There are few signs of insecurity. Extra enforcement officers are not needed now; adjustments should be made if circumstances change.

122361.explanation: In case of housing shortages, temporary residence can prevent homelessness. As long as there are insufficient suitable homes available, we find it unreasonable to prohibit this practice without offering an alternative. Temporary residence can therefore be a solution, provided it is well regulated.

122363.explanation: Participation is important, but not everyone is able to do this. A reciprocal obligation must be voluntary and appropriate and only make sense if tailored to what someone can handle. Customization and guidance take priority, not an obligation to meet a standard rule.

122365.explanation: By raising the income threshold, more residents can receive timely support. This helps to prevent financial problems and debts rather than having to resolve them afterwards. Early detection and assistance fit a caring and sensible municipality.

122367.explanation: More and more residents drive electric cars and sufficient charging points are needed for this. We find it important to grow along with this, based on a good inventory of current and future needs. This way we prevent both shortages and unnecessary loss of parking spaces.

122368.explanation: Many places in Renswoude already have a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h. On streets such as Dorpsstraat, this can help discourage rat-running and improve traffic safety. We choose tailor-made solutions per location, not a generic measure without effect.

122369.explanation: We find it important that people can receive care from a care provider that aligns with their faith, culture, or belief. This increases trust and well-being. At the same time, we must also consider affordability and practical feasibility within the healthcare system.

122379.explanation: De Hokhorst is an important facility for sports and socializing in Renswoude. Plans for renewal are already in place and currently being developed. We support this development to make De Hokhorst future-proof.

122382.explanation: Not in 'Renswouws' language but indeed related to the history of our beautiful village. Fortunately, this is already being offered.

122386.explanation: Renting out single rooms gives young people extra chances for housing in times of shortage. Room rental is already allowed but under clear rules. As far as we’re concerned, this option should remain and efforts made for more flexible rules so good initiatives don't get stuck.

122388.explanation: 14-18 year olds often fall between the cracks with events. The existing celebrations in the village are large, accessible, and highly valued; therefore, we support new and small-scale initiatives such as game or FIFA evenings, sports tournaments, and alcohol-free youth activities!

122390.explanation: Both groups struggle in the tight housing market. By carefully looking at housing needs and building accordingly, we ensure a good distribution and retention of Renswoude families for all generations.

122332.explanation: We aim for the best candidates, regardless of gender. Besides, with a small board of three people, there is never equal representation.

122333.explanation: CDA Renswoude sees no value in a referendum. We prefer to engage directly with residents.

122334.explanation: CDA Renswoude is always open to initiatives from society. We like to discuss possibilities and make appropriate agreements regarding initiatives.

122336.explanation: CDA Renswoude is always open to secondary functions at farms. However, the primary function must remain agricultural.

122337.explanation: CDA Renswoude believes that society and the municipality should work together on energy matters.

122341.explanation: We do not trade these off against each other in this way. However, we do believe Renswoude should remain a rural municipality with sufficient green spaces.

122345.explanation: At the moment, bulky waste can already be collected at home for free. This then costs points from the waste pass. It only costs money if there are no points left on the waste pass. We want to keep the situation as it is.

122346.explanation: CDA Renswoude sees no reason to impose stricter standards by law.

122348.explanation: CDA Renswoude believes space should be offered to farmers to build houses. However, we would prefer that existing buildings of retiring farmers can be repurposed. This concerns buildings only, not the surrounding land.

122349.explanation: For children, the costs of sports club membership can be paid from the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture if the parents cannot afford it. We want to keep it that way.

122351.explanation: CDA Renswoude does not want to invest directly in school buildings but first wants to investigate whether they are future-proof and can handle village growth. Afterwards, we want to plan for sustainable and appropriate primary schools.

122352.explanation: If the parents cannot pay, an application can be made to the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture. We want to keep it that way.

122356.explanation: We find enforcement of regulations important. This does not necessarily mean more enforcement officers need to be deployed.

122361.explanation: Because of the housing shortage, CDA Renswoude sees allowing this as an option.

122363.explanation: We would like to see people contribute valuably to society, but cannot make that a requirement for welfare benefits.

122365.explanation: CDA Renswoude wants to keep the regulations as they are. If it turns out more help is needed, we are happy to discuss and explore possibilities.

122367.explanation: CDA Renswoude believes more charging points for electric cars should not be designated hastily. It must align with demand. Given the growth of electric cars, this is probably inevitable.

122368.explanation: CDA Renswoude wants to ban heavy traffic within the built-up area and lower the speed limit to 30 km/h to improve traffic safety.

122369.explanation: In our view, the municipality has little influence here without healthcare institutions. We appreciate the caregivers and assume they treat everyone well regardless of faith, culture, or beliefs.

122379.explanation: CDA Renswoude believes there should be a vision of how Renswoude looks in 20 years. To guarantee long-term housing, it may be necessary to create space within the built-up area. We do not rule out relocating sports fields to create that space.

122380.explanation: The plans for the community center mainly consist of wishful thinking by the current board. Projects like De Breehoek in Scherpenzeel are often not a resounding success. We are also critical of the high costs and ask for attention to the sustainability of the sports hall.

122382.explanation: It is not directly about the Renswous language, but we find it valuable for primary school students to come into contact with the local culture.

122386.explanation: To combat the housing shortage, creative solutions are needed. For example, we want to allow space for house splitting or shared housing. Renting out single rooms is also among the options.

122388.explanation: CDA Renswoude would like to see more events organized for youth. If initiatives arise from society, we want to think along and support where possible. However, the opportunity to organize events independently is limited.

122390.explanation: CDA Renswoude wants to build according to the village's needs. Currently, this means many homes for starters and the elderly are being built.

122332.explanation: OPRenswoude stands for equal opportunities and appointments based on quality and suitability, not on gender. The board must consist of the best people for Renswoude, without fixed quotas or imposed distribution.

122333.explanation: OPRenswoude stands for a strong representative democracy. Residents should be able to express their opinions, but referenda can only be advisory. Decisions remain with the elected council, which is responsible for careful consideration of complex issues.

122334.explanation: OPRenswoude believes that basic services remain the responsibility of the municipality to guarantee quality and accessibility. If residents want to take over management and it can be done responsibly, the municipality facilitates this with clear agreements, support, and supervision.

122336.explanation: OPRenswoude stands for space and trust for farmers, but not for automatic approval. Secondary activities are possible as long as they fit the agricultural character, the landscape, and the livability of the environment. Custom solutions and clear conditions remain necessary.

122337.explanation: OPRenswoude stands for freedom and initiative. Sustainable energy projects by residents deserve space and clear rules, but structural financial support from the municipality does not align with a liberal course. The municipality's role is facilitating, not subsidizing.

122341.explanation: OPRenswoude values greenery for a pleasant and livable village. By planting a new tree for every one cut down, the green character of Renswoude is preserved and nature is responsibly managed during development and maintenance.

122345.explanation: OPRenswoude believes waste collection should be cost-covering. Free bulky waste collection leads to higher costs for everyone. The municipality must provide accessible recycling centers and tailor-made solutions for those who genuinely need help.

122346.explanation: OPRenswoude believes village festivals and events belong to community life. Stricter noise standards are unnecessary. However, nuisance and hearing damage should be limited through clear agreements, end times, and responsible use—not through additional rules.

122348.explanation: OPRenswoude wants to keep the rural area open and agricultural. Housing on farmland causes fragmentation and does not fit Renswoude. Construction should take place in designated locations. Exceptions such as care homes or 'Space-for-Space' remain possible.

122349.explanation: OPRenswoude considers participation important. Sports help people stay healthy and actively involved in village life. For residents receiving welfare benefits, support via existing schemes may be necessary so financial barriers do not hinder participation.

122351.explanation: OPRenswoude believes in good and safe school buildings. Investments are needed where quality, safety, or sustainability fall short. This must be done targeted and phased, considering necessity, affordability, and proper management.

122352.explanation: OPRenswoude values music education but finds subsidies for every child excessive. Support should be targeted to those who need it. The municipality encourages culture through associations and schools without new structural subsidies.

122356.explanation: OPRenswoude considers safety important but believes more enforcement officers are not automatically the solution. The focus should be on visible police presence, the community officer, and good cooperation with residents. Enforcement should be effective and purposeful, not just bigger for the sake of it.

122361.explanation: OPRenswoude wants to maintain the distinction between living and recreation. Permanent residency in holiday homes leads to unwanted mixing of functions and extra pressure on facilities. Housing shortages require targeted housing construction, not exceptions.

122363.explanation: OPRenswoude sees welfare as a springboard, not a hammock. Where possible, socially meaningful work is appropriate and helps with participation and moving on. This should always be tailored, with attention to health, caregiving duties, and possibilities.

122365.explanation: OPRenswoude wants to deploy assistance specifically for residents who truly need it. Raising income limits makes regulations less targeted and more expensive. Tailoring and individual assessment better fit a pragmatic and fair poverty policy.

122367.explanation: OPRenswoude wants to enable electric driving but not at the cost of scarce parking spaces. The focus is first on charging on private premises and at companies. Public charging points only where necessary, based on demand-driven placement, not fixed standards.

122368.explanation: OPRenswoude chooses traffic safety with common sense. 30 km/h is appropriate in residential streets and near schools, but not as a general measure. Speeds must be logical and fit the function of the road and the traffic situation.

122369.explanation: OPRenswoude finds it important that care providers handle faith and culture respectfully. Where possible, patients' wishes should be taken into account. However, this is not always feasible; quality, availability, and affordability of care remain paramount.

122379.explanation: OPRenswoude considers good sports facilities important. Renewal of sports park De Hokhorst is possible where needed for safety, maintenance, and use. This must be pragmatic, phased, and affordable, with attention to costs and support.

122380.explanation: OPRenswoude believes that the new Village Hall and music building Het Podium already provide sufficient cultural facilities. An additional cultural center is unnecessary. First, existing facilities should be well utilized and strengthened.

122382.explanation: OPRenswoude finds it important that children learn about the history, traditions, and identity of Renswoude. Attention to Renswoude heritage at school strengthens bonds with the village and respect for the local community.

122386.explanation: OPRenswoude wants to protect livability in residential neighborhoods. Renting out individual rooms can lead to extra pressure on housing, parking problems, and nuisance. Shared housing is not a structural solution for housing shortages and requires clear limits.

122388.explanation: OPRenswoude finds it important that young people feel at home in the village. The municipality should enable and support initiatives from youth and associations. Events preferably originate from the grassroots, fitting with support and livability.

122390.explanation: OPRenswoude wants starters and seniors to be able to stay living in Renswoude. Prioritizing these groups in new housing triggers movement and keeps homes available for families, fitting the scale and needs of the village.

statements.title: More events should be organized for young people in Renswoude.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

titleParts.text: worden georganiseerd voor jongeren in Renswoude.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Renswoude should renovate sports park De Hokhorst.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Renswoude should build a new cultural center in the town center.
The residents of Renswoude should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Renswoude moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: In new construction, housing for first-time buyers and the elderly should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: People buying a house for the first time who have never had a mortgage.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Renswous dialect.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: Renswoude should deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
